mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing present values of future cash flows and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window or forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.10) during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.4 percentage points signals sudden labor market deterioration, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns within the following week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances over a 24-month backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand strength, driving corporate revenue growth expectations and positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: Joint positive signals yield negative 60-day forward returns in over 60% of cases, or interaction term loses statistical significance in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth accelerates above 1.5% quarter-over-quarter during late-cycle expansions, signaling capacity utilization peaks that precede margin compression and negative equity returns.
failure_condition: Signal fails to predict negative returns during late-cycle phases, with out-of-sample accuracy dropping below 45% over a 5-year evaluation period.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls reflect strengthening economic activity and higher corporate earnings trajectories, generating positive short-term momentum in S&P 500 prices.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains negative or below 0.01 across all 12-month rolling windows, or payroll gains consistently coincide with negative 5-day equity returns.

mechanism_narrative: Core inflation measured by CPIAUCSL breaching 2.8% forces central banks to accelerate tightening, raising discount rates and compressing equity valuations over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of threshold crossings, or inflation spikes fail to precede monetary tightening within 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising real GDP and falling real personal consumption expenditures indicates investment-led growth that lacks consumer support, creating earnings uncertainty and negative monthly equity returns.
failure_condition: Divergence signals produce positive forward returns in over 55% of instances, or the interaction effect becomes statistically insignificant in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production declines during post-shock recovery phases signal persistent supply chain bottlenecks that delay earnings rebounds, causing immediate negative equity price adjustments.
failure_condition: Negative industrial production signals fail to predict 5-day returns during recovery phases, with directional accuracy falling below 40% over a 3-year walk-forward test.
